dialogueChunks.intro: She walked through the gate this morning, {player_name}. I saw her from the window and nearly fell over.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: Three seasons apart, and the first thing she did was apologize. We stood in the doorway crying while the whole settlement watched. {target_npc} brought flowers. {location} feels different now: fuller. {quest_focus} is finally home, and {quest_stakes} is over.
dialogueChunks.outro: You brought my sister home. No amount of coin can equal that, but please take this.

playerResponses.text: Seeing you together again is worth more than any payment.
A missing piece back in place. That's all that matters.
Hold onto her this time. Don't let pride or shame separate you again.

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}... the trail led somewhere I didn't expect. Somewhere wonderful.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {target_npc} was alive the whole time: living under a different name, working as a craftsperson. They'd started over and found peace. When I found them, they wept from the shock that someone never stopped searching. They're not ready to come back to {location}, but they promised to visit. {quest_focus} is resolved: {quest_stakes} was answered with life, not death.
dialogueChunks.outro: You made this reunion possible. Take this with all my heart.

playerResponses.text: A new life isn't a betrayal. It's survival. I'm glad they're well.
Knowing they're alive changes everything. Be patient with the rest.

dialogueChunks.intro: They're free, {player_name}. {target_npc} walked out of that camp and didn't look back.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {quest_threat} took the {quest_item} and honored the deal. {target_npc} arrived at {location} last night, thinner and quieter, but alive. They slept fourteen hours straight. This morning they sat in the garden watching birds, like they'd forgotten what stillness felt like. {quest_focus} ended with freedom. {quest_stakes} is finally over.
dialogueChunks.outro: You brought someone back from the edge of being lost forever. This reward is everything I could gather.

playerResponses.text: Watching birds in a garden. That's what freedom looks like.
Give them time and space. Recovery isn't instant.
They're home. That's the only reward that counts.
